column2: Test Case Title
column4: Test Steps
column5: Expected Result

column2: Verify login screen UI elements
column4:
1. Open login page
2. Inspect all elements on screen
column5: All elements are present: Username field, Password field, Sign in button, Remember password checkbox, Forgot password link, Create an account link

column2: Verify font style and size visibility
column4:
1. Inspect font style and size of labels and text on all objects
2. Check readability
column5: Font style and size are consistent, clear, and readable as per specifications

column2: Verify UI elements size, color, and design
column3: Login page is loaded, UI specifications available
column4:
1. Compare actual UI elements with design specifications
2. Check size, color, and overall design
column5: All elements match the specified size, color, and design requirements

column2: Verify responsive UI on different screen resolutions
column4:
1. Open login page on different devices/screen resolutions
2. Check layout and element alignment
column5: UI adapts correctly to different screen resolutions and devices without breaking

column2: Verify placeholder text in fields
column4:
1. Inspect Username field placeholder
2. Inspect Password field placeholder
column5: Both fields have valid placeholder text indicating expected input

column2: Verify password masking
column4:
1. Enter text in Password field
2. Observe how password is displayed
column5: Password is displayed in masked form (bullets or asterisks)

column2: Verify cursor focus on Username field
column4:
1. Open login page
2. Observe where cursor is focused
column5: Cursor is automatically focused on the Username text box on page load

column2: Verify tab functionality
column4:
1. Press Tab key repeatedly
2. Observe focus movement between elements
column5: Tab key moves focus in logical order through all interactive elements

column2: Verify Enter/Tab key substitutes for Sign-in button
column3: Login page is loaded, valid credentials available
column4:
1. Enter valid credentials
2. Press Enter key (or Tab then Enter)
3. Observe system response
column5: System processes login as if Sign-in button was clicked

column2: Verify minimum character length for Username field
column4:
1. Enter username with fewer characters than the minimum required length (e.g., min length = 3, enter "ab")
2. Enter valid password
3. Click Sign-in button or Tab out of the field
column5: Validation message appears indicating the username must meet the minimum length requirement; login is prevented

column2: Verify maximum character length for Username field
column4:
1. Enter one more character beyond the maximum limit.
2. Enter valid password
3. Click Sign-in button or Tab out of the field
column5: System prevents entering beyond maximum limit; validation message appears OR field does not accept additional characters

column2: Verify minimum character length for Password field
column4:
1. Enter valid username
2. Enter password with fewer characters than the minimum required length (e.g., min length = 6, enter "12345")
3. Click Sign-in button or Tab out of the field
column5: Validation message appears indicating the password must meet the minimum length requirement; login is prevented

column2: Verify maximum character length for Password field
column4:
1. Enter valid username
2. Enter password one with more character beyond the maximum limit.
3. Click Sign-in button or Tab out of the field
column5: System prevents entering beyond maximum limit; validation message appears OR field does not accept additional characters

column2: Verify boundary value for Username field - exactly minimum length
column3: Login page is loaded, valid credentials with username exactly at minimum length exist
column4:
1. Enter username with exactly the minimum required characters (e.g., min length = 3, enter "abc")
2. Enter valid password
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Username is accepted; login processes normally (success or further validation)

column2: Verify boundary value for Username field - exactly maximum length
column3: Login page is loaded, valid credentials with username exactly at maximum length exist
column4:
1. Enter username with exactly the maximum allowed characters (e.g., max length = 50)
2. Enter valid password
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Username is accepted; login processes normally (success or further validation)

column2: Verify boundary value for Password field - exactly minimum length
column3: Login page is loaded, valid credentials with password exactly at minimum length exist
column4:
1. Enter valid username.
2. Enter password with exactly the minimum required characters (e.g., min length = 6, enter "123456")
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Password is accepted; login processes normally (success or further validation)

column2: Verify boundary value for Password field - exactly maximum length
column3: Login page is loaded, valid credentials with password exactly at maximum length exist
column4:
1. Enter valid username
2. Enter password with exactly the maximum allowed characters (e.g., max length = 20)
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Password is accepted; login processes normally (success or further validation)

column2: Verify copy-paste behavior respects maximum length limits
column4:
1. Copy a string longer than the maximum allowed length
2. Paste it into Username field
column5: System either prevents pasting beyond limit, truncates the input, or shows validation message

column2: Verify password copy-paste restriction
column4:
1. Copy vaild password string (Ctrl+C or right-click)
3. Attempt to paste copied password
column5: System prevents pasting into password field.

column2: Verify encrypted password cannot be deciphered if copied
column4:
1. Attempt to copy password using any method
2. Check if copied content reveals actual password
column5: Copied password shows only masked characters; actual password cannot be deciphered

column2: Verify cross-browser compatibility
column3: Login page URL, multiple browsers installed
column4:
1. Open login page in different browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
2. Check display and functionality
column5: Login page displays correctly and functions properly in all supported browsers

column2: Verify successful login with valid credentials
column4:
1. Enter valid username
2. Enter valid password
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: User is successfully logged in and redirected to appropriate page (e.g., dashboard/home page)

column2: Verify login with invalid username and invalid password
column4:
1. Enter invalid username
2. Enter invalid password
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Login fails; appropriate error message displayed

column2: Verify login with valid username and invalid password
column4:
1. Enter valid username
2. Enter invalid password
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Login fails; appropriate error message displayed (should not specify which field is incorrect)

column2: Verify login with invalid username and valid password
column4:
1. Enter invalid username
2. Enter valid password
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Login fails; appropriate error message displayed

column2: Verify login with blank Username and valid Password
column3: Login page is loaded, valid password available
column4:
1. Leave Username field empty (blank)
2. Enter valid password in Password field
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Validation message appears indicating "Username is required" or "Please enter username"; login is prevented

column2: Verify login with valid Username and blank Password
column3: Login page is loaded, valid username available
column4:
1. Enter valid username in Username field
2. Leave Password field empty (blank) 
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Validation message appears indicating "Password is required" or "Please enter password"; login is prevented

column2: Verify login with blank Username and blank Password
column4:
1. Leave Username field empty (blank)
2. Leave Password field empty (blank)
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Validation message(s) appear indicating both fields are required; login is prevented

column2: Verify login with whitespace only in Username field
column3: Login page is loaded, valid password available
column4:
1. Enter only spaces in Username field (e.g., " ")
2. Enter valid password 
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: System treats whitespace as blank; validation message appears indicating "Username is required"; login is prevented

column2: Verify login with whitespace only in Password field
column3: Login page is loaded, valid username available
column4:
1. Enter valid username
2. Enter only spaces in Password field (e.g., " ")
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: System treats whitespace as blank; validation message appears indicating "Password is required"; login is prevented

column2: Verify validation message style and placement for blank fields
column4:
1. Leave both fields blank
2. Click Sign-in button
3. Observe validation message style, color, font, and placement
column5: Validation messages are clearly visible, consistent with UI design, placed near the relevant fields (e.g., below each field or in a banner)

column2: Verify login with inactive credentials
column4:
1. Enter inactive username
2. Enter corresponding password
3. Click Sign-in button
column5: Login fails; appropriate message indicating account is inactive or disabled

column2: Verify successful login after account recovery
column3: Account recovery completed, new credentials available
column4:
1. Perform account recovery process
2. Login with recovered/new credentials
column5: User can successfully login with recovered credentials

column2: Verify login with username containing uppercase and lowercase
column3: Valid username with mixed case, valid password
column4:
1. Enter username with uppercase and lowercase letters
2. Enter password
3. Click Sign-in
column5: Login successful if username is case-insensitive (or fails if case-sensitive as per business logic)

column2: Verify login with username containing special characters
column3: Valid username with special characters (@, #, $, etc.), valid password
column4:
1. Enter username with special characters
2. Enter password
3. Click Sign-in
column5: System handles special characters appropriately; login successful if username is valid

column2: Verify spaces not allowed before username characters
column4:
1. Enter username with leading spaces
2. Attempt login
column5: System should not allow spaces before username characters; either trim or reject with validation

column2: Verify spaces not allowed after username characters
column4:
1. Enter username with trailing spaces
2. Attempt login
column5: System should not allow spaces after username characters; either trim or reject with validation

column2: Verify that login error messages are clear and do not reveal username existence
column3: Login page is loaded, valid and invalid usernames
column4:
1. Attempt login with existing username and wrong password
2. Attempt login with non-existing username
3. Compare error messages
column5: Both attempts show the same generic error message (e.g., "Invalid username or password"). The message is clear to the user but does NOT reveal whether the username exists in the system

column2: Verify password encryption in database
column4:
1. Create a new user or check existing user
2. Open database connection and navigate to users table
3. Run SELECT query to retrieve the stored password for the newly created user
4. Compare the stored value with the original plain text password
column5: Password is stored in encrypted format (not plain text)

column2: Verify login form does not reveal security info in page source
column4:
1. Right-click and view page source
2. Search for sensitive information (passwords, tokens, etc.)
column5: No sensitive security information is exposed in page source

column2: Verify login with new password after password change
column4:
1. Change password successfully
2. Login with new password
column5: User can successfully login with the new password

column2: Verify login with old password after password change
column4:
1. Change password successfully
2. Attempt login with old password
column5: Login fails; user cannot login with old password

column2: Verify "Remember Me" checkbox default state
column4:
1. Open login page
2. Observe Remember Me checkbox state
column5: Checkbox is un-selected by default (or as per business logic)

column2: Verify successful login with "Remember Me" selected
column4:
1. Select Remember Me checkbox
2. Enter valid credentials 
3. Click Sign-in
4. Close and reopen browser/application
column5: User remains logged in or credentials are remembered as per business logic

column2: Verify Forgot Password link redirection
column4: 1. Click Forgot Password link
column5: User is redirected to Forgot Password page

column2: Verify Create Account link redirection
column4: 1. Click Create an account / Signup link
column5: User is redirected to account creation/registration page

column2: Verify reset button clears form data
column3: Login page is loaded, fields contain data
column4:
1. Enter data in Username and Password fields
2. Click Reset button
column5: All data in Username and Password fields is cleared

column2: Verify session timeout
column4:
1. Login successfully
2. Leave application idle for the configured timeout period
3. Attempt to perform an action
column5: Session expires; user is redirected to login page or prompted to re-authenticate

column2: Verify session persistence after browser close and reopen
column4:
1. Login successfully with Remember Me if applicable
2. Close browser completely
3. Reopen browser and access application
column5: User session state depends on Remember Me functionality; session may persist or require re-login as per security requirements

column2: Verify browser back button after logout
column4:
1. Login and then logout successfully
2. Click browser back button
column5: User is not taken to logged-in mode; session is terminated

column2: Verify unsuccessful login attempt limit
column4:
1. Attempt login with invalid credentials multiple times
2. Reach configured limit of attempts
column5: After limit reached, user is blocked, prompted for CAPTCHA, or locked out temporarily as per business logic

column2: Verify SQL injection vulnerability
column4:
1. Attempt SQL injection attacks in Username field (e.g., ' OR '1'='1)
2. Attempt SQL injection attacks in Password field
column5: System prevents SQL injection; login fails and no database compromise occurs

column2: Verify XSS vulnerability on login page
column4: 1. Attempt XSS script injection in input fields (e.g., <script>alert('XSS')</script>)
column5: System sanitizes input; scripts are not executed

column2: Verify performance across browsers and devices
column4:
1. Test login functionality on different browsers
2. Test login functionality on different devices (desktop, tablet, mobile)
column5: Login page performs consistently and responsively across all tested browsers and devices

column2: Verify concurrent login restriction
column4:
1. Login with Account A in one browser tab/window
2. Attempt login with Account B in same browser but different tab/window
column5: System should handle concurrent sessions appropriately; may allow or restrict based on business logic

column2: Verify SSL certificate implementation
column4:
1. Access login page via HTTPS
2. Verify SSL certificate validity
column5: SSL certificate is valid and properly implemented; connection is secure

column2: Verify localization settings
column4:
1. Set browser/application to different language
2. Open login page
3. Perform login
column5: All UI elements display correctly in selected language; login works properly
